summary:
The child should assume either the knee-chest or prone position during a tet spell due to its effectiveness at alleviating symptoms associated with oxygen deprivation typical for those suffering from Tetralogy of Fallot. This positioning aids in improving circulation and reducing cyanosis quickly when necessary interventions are required urgently. Agent1 reasoned based primarily on managing acute hypoxia events through appropriate body positioning known to help these patients temporarily until medical assistance arrives. 

The summary captures both core aspects requested—specifically mentioning the recommended postures while also touching upon their purpose related directly back to symptom relief pertinent within pediatric cardiology practice guidelines concerning TOF care emergencies.
